chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI. C.P No. D-4901 of 2025 ____________________________________________________________ Dated Order with signature of Judge. ____________________________________________________________ Priority 1. For order on office objection 2. For order on CMA No.24889/2025 3. For order on CMA No.21076/2025 4. For Order on Nazir's Report Dt.10.11.2025 5. For hearing of CMA No.20472/2025 6. For hearing of CMA No.21814/2025 7. For hearing of main case 28.01.2026. Mr. Mirza Moiz Baig, Advocate for the Petitioner Mr. M. Maz Waheed, Advocate for Respondent No.7. Mr. Faiz Durrani, Advocate for Respondent No.8. Ms. Umaira Jatoi, Advocate for Sindh Master Plan/SBCA a/w Mr. Rafique Ahmed Bhanbhro, Mr. Kuldeep Kumar, Advocate holds brief for Mr. Ahmed Ali Hussain, Advocate for Respondent No.9. Mr. Khursheed Javed, Advocate for KDA. Mr. Muhammad Akbar Khan, A.A.G. Mr. Muhammad Hasham, Asstt. Advocate General. --------------- The case of the Petitioner is directed against the establishment of two petrol pumps in close proximity to one another, which according to the Petitioner is in violation of regulation 25-11.4 of the Karachi Building and Town Planning Regulations-2002. The Petition accordingly assails the NOC granted for establishment one of those pumps, with the crux of the Petitioner’s case being that both cannot co-exist. However, it transpires from the comments submitted in the matter on behalf of the Respondent No.4 that the NOC granted to the Respondent No.9 has since been withdrawn. The relevant Order dated 26.02.2024 issued by the Sindh Master Plan Authority has also been attached alongwith such comments. Furthermore it is stated by counsel in attendance that certain Civil Suits relating to

The relevant Order dated 26.02.2024 issued by the Sindh Master Plan Authority has also been attached alongwith such comments. Furthermore it is stated by counsel in attendance that certain Civil Suits relating to the pumps remain pending. Under such circumstances, where the NOC has been withdrawn, the purpose of the Petition appears to have been served, while the further issues sought to be agitated by the parties fall to be considered in those civil proceedings. As such, the Petition stands disposed of accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE TariqAli/PS
